features.name: Stage 2 dispatches BDD and TLA+ writers in parallel

scenarios.name: Both writers receive the elicitor briefing as sole input

steps.text: feature "auth-flow" has a completed elicitor briefing at "docs/auth-flow/elicitor.md"
the BDD writer receives "docs/auth-flow/elicitor.md" as its input
the TLA+ writer receives "docs/auth-flow/elicitor.md" as its input
neither writer receives the other writer's output

scenarios.name: Both writers run concurrently via Invoke-Parallel

steps.text: feature "auth-flow" has a completed elicitor briefing
Invoke-Parallel is called with two jobs: "bdd" and "tla"

scenarios.name: BDD writer produces bdd.feature

steps.text: the BDD writer completes successfully for feature "auth-flow"
the file contains valid Gherkin syntax

scenarios.name: TLA+ writer produces spec files with TLC verification

steps.text: the TLA+ writer completes successfully for feature "auth-flow"
at least one .tla file exists under "docs/auth-flow/tla/"
TLC verification passed as part of the writer's process

scenarios.name: One writer fails while the other succeeds

steps.text: feature "auth-flow" has a completed elicitor briefing
the BDD writer will fail during execution
stage 2 dispatches both writers in parallel
the pipeline waits for the TLA+ writer to complete (does not kill it)
stage 2 fails with an error reporting that the BDD writer failed
"docs/auth-flow/tla/" contains the TLA+ writer's output (preserved on disk)

scenarios.name: Both writers fail

steps.text: feature "auth-flow" has a completed elicitor briefing
both writers will fail during execution
stage 2 dispatches both writers in parallel
stage 2 fails with an error reporting that both the BDD writer and TLA+ writer failed

scenarios.name: TLA+ writer fails while BDD writer succeeds

steps.text: feature "auth-flow" has a completed elicitor briefing
the TLA+ writer will fail during execution
stage 2 dispatches both writers in parallel
the pipeline waits for the BDD writer to complete (does not kill it)
stage 2 fails with an error reporting that the TLA+ writer failed

scenarios.name: Stage 2 writes a completion marker on success

steps.text: feature "auth-flow" has a completed elicitor briefing

features.name: Invoke-Parallel dispatches scriptblocks as concurrent jobs with keyed results

scenarios.name: Two jobs both succeed and return keyed results

steps.text: Invoke-Parallel is called with jobs "alpha" and "beta"
the result hashtable contains key "alpha" with job alpha's output
the result hashtable contains key "beta" with job beta's output

scenarios.name: One job fails and the other succeeds with typed error result

steps.text: Invoke-Parallel is called with jobs "alpha" and "beta"
job "alpha" throws an error with message "Claude invocation timed out"

scenarios.name: Both jobs fail with typed error results

steps.text: Invoke-Parallel is called with jobs "alpha" and "beta"
job "alpha" throws an error with message "alpha error"
job "beta" throws an error with message "beta error"

scenarios.name: Results are keyed by the caller-provided job name

steps.text: Invoke-Parallel is called with jobs "bdd" and "tla"
both jobs return different output values

scenarios.name: Invoke-Parallel is used within the debate loop for parallel revisions

steps.text: the unified debate loop has objections for both "bdd" and "tla" writers
Invoke-Parallel is called with a "bdd" revision job and a "tla" revision job

scenarios.name: Parallel jobs do not corrupt pipeline.log with concurrent writes

steps.text: Invoke-Parallel is called with jobs "alpha" and "beta"
both jobs call Write-PipelineLog during execution
both jobs write to pipeline.log concurrently
each log line is written atomically (no interleaved characters)
Write-PipelineLog uses a mutex or file lock to serialize writes

scenarios.name: Job exceeding timeout is terminated

steps.text: Invoke-Parallel is called with jobs "alpha" and "beta" and a timeout of 300 seconds

features.name: Unified debate reviews both BDD and TLA+ documents together

scenarios.name: Moderator receives both documents for review

steps.text: the unified debate loop starts for "auth-flow"
the debate moderator agent receives both the BDD feature file and the TLA+ spec file
all participating experts see both documents

scenarios.name: Consensus requires both documents approved simultaneously

steps.text: the moderator has reviewed both documents
the moderator has no objections for the BDD document
the moderator has no objections for the TLA+ document

scenarios.name: Partial consensus when only one document has objections

steps.text: the moderator has reviewed both documents
the moderator has no objections for the BDD document
the moderator has 2 objections for the TLA+ document
the debate loop continues (no partial graduation)

scenarios.name: Objections are tagged with target field for routing

steps.text: the moderator produces objections in round 3
each objection has a "target" field with value "bdd" or "tla"
objections are grouped by target before dispatching to writers

scenarios.name: Single-writer objections route only to that writer

steps.text: the moderator has 3 objections all tagged with target "tla"
the moderator has 0 objections tagged with target "bdd"
only the TLA+ writer receives revision instructions
the BDD writer is not invoked in this round
the pipeline waits for the TLA+ revision to complete before the next round

scenarios.name: Both-writer objections dispatch parallel revisions

steps.text: the moderator has 2 objections tagged with target "bdd"
the moderator has 1 objection tagged with target "tla"
the BDD writer and TLA+ writer are invoked in parallel via Invoke-Parallel
the pipeline waits for both revisions to complete before the next round

scenarios.name: TLC verification runs as part of TLA+ revision

steps.text: the TLA+ writer receives a revision instruction
the TLA+ writer completes its revision
TLC verification runs as part of the revision process
the revision is only considered "done" when TLC passes

scenarios.name: Debate session is captured in unified-debate.md

steps.text: the unified debate loop runs for 4 rounds
the session file includes all rounds, objections, and revisions for both documents

scenarios.name: Consensus reached triggers per-writer final recommendations

steps.text: the debate loop processes the consensus result
the BDD writer receives the recommendation keyed under "bdd"
the TLA+ writer receives the recommendation keyed under "tla"
both final revisions are applied before the loop exits

scenarios.name: Max debate rounds (10) reached with unresolved objections

steps.text: the unified debate loop has completed 10 rounds
the moderator still has unresolved objections
the pipeline proceeds to stage 4 with the current document versions

scenarios.name: Invoke-UnifiedDebateLoop returns structured result to stage 3

steps.text: the unified debate loop completes with consensus in round 4
the return object contains .Result with value "CONSENSUS_REACHED"

scenarios.name: Invoke-UnifiedDebateLoop returns unresolved objections when max rounds hit

steps.text: the unified debate loop exits after 10 rounds without consensus

scenarios.name: Stage 3 writes a completion marker on success

steps.text: the unified debate loop completes for feature "auth-flow"

scenarios.name: Resume replays stage 3 entirely (round counter resets)

steps.text: the pipeline crashed during stage 3 round 6 for feature "auth-flow"
Resume-Pipeline detects last completed stage as 2
stage 3 restarts from round 1 with a fresh 10-round budget
the prior partial unified-debate.md is overwritten

features.name: Debate loop handles revision failures gracefully

scenarios.name: BDD revision fails during a debate round

steps.text: the debate loop dispatched parallel revisions for both writers
the BDD writer's revision fails (e.g., Claude invocation error)
the TLA+ writer's revision is preserved on disk
the debate round fails with an error logging the BDD revision failure

scenarios.name: TLA+ revision fails due to TLC check exhaustion

steps.text: the debate loop dispatched a revision for the TLA+ writer
the TLA+ writer exhausts all TLC check attempts
the debate round fails with an error logging the TLC exhaustion
any successful BDD revision from the same round is preserved on disk

scenarios.name: Both revisions fail in the same round

steps.text: the debate loop dispatched parallel revisions for both writers
the debate round fails with an error logging both failures

scenarios.name: Revision failure preserves prior successful revisions

steps.text: the debate loop completed rounds 1 and 2 successfully
in round 3 the TLA+ revision fails
"docs/auth-flow/tla/" reflects the round 2 TLA+ revision (latest successful)

features.name: Unified debate moderator outputs structured JSON with per-writer fields

scenarios.name: Moderator output contains required fields

steps.text: the unified debate moderator completes a round
it contains a "result" field with value "CONSENSUS_REACHED" or "PARTIAL_CONSENSUS"
it contains an "experts" array listing participating experts
it contains a "recommendation" object with "bdd" and "tla" keys
it contains a "sessionFile" field set to "unified-debate.md"

scenarios.name: Each objection has a target field

steps.text: each objection object has a "target" field with value "bdd" or "tla"
each objection object has an "objection" field with the critique text

scenarios.name: Recommendation is split per writer at consensus

steps.text: recommendation.bdd contains final guidance for the BDD writer
recommendation.tla contains final guidance for the TLA+ writer

scenarios.name: Empty objections array at consensus
Partial consensus includes at least one objection

steps.text: the objections array contains at least one objection

features.name: Stage 4 generates BDD fixture and post-debate artifacts

scenarios.name: BDD fixture is generated from final bdd.feature

steps.text: the unified debate has completed for feature "auth-flow"
the fixture JSON is parsed from the final Gherkin using ConvertFrom-Gherkin and Export-BddFixture

scenarios.name: Fixture JSON is written atomically

steps.text: stage 4 is generating the BDD fixture for feature "auth-flow"
the fixture is written to disk
the write uses atomic write (temp file then Move-Item -Force)
readers never see partial fixture content

scenarios.name: Stage 4 fails if bdd.feature is missing

steps.text: the unified debate completed but "docs/auth-flow/bdd.feature" does not exist
stage 4 attempts to generate the fixture
stage 4 fails with an error indicating the BDD feature file is missing

scenarios.name: Stage 4 fails if bdd.feature contains invalid Gherkin

steps.text: stage 4 attempts to parse and generate the fixture
stage 4 fails with a Gherkin parse error

scenarios.name: Stage 4 writes a completion marker on success

steps.text: stage 4 generates the fixture for feature "auth-flow"

features.name: vibe.ps1 uses -Resume switch instead of -Stage and -Feature parameters

scenarios.name: Fresh run with $Seed starts from stage 1

steps.text: the user runs "./vibe.ps1 'Build an auth module'"
stage 1 (elicitor) begins with the seed "Build an auth module"

scenarios.name: -Resume auto-detects feature and last completed stage

steps.text: pipeline.log records that feature "auth-flow" completed through stage 3
Resume-Pipeline detects feature "auth-flow" from the log
Resume-Pipeline detects last completed stage as 3

scenarios.name: -Resume requires no other arguments

steps.text: the user runs "./vibe.ps1 -Resume" with no additional parameters
the feature name is auto-detected from the log
the resume stage is auto-detected from the log

scenarios.name: Fresh run requires $Seed

steps.text: the user runs "./vibe.ps1" with no arguments
the pipeline fails because $Seed is required for a fresh run

scenarios.name: -Stage and -Feature parameters are removed
Resume detects correct stage with 7-stage numbering

steps.text: pipeline.log records stage completions using the new 7-stage numbering
the last completed stage marker is for stage 5 (implementation-writer)

scenarios.name: -Resume and $Seed together fails parameter validation

steps.text: the user runs "./vibe.ps1 'Build an auth module' -Resume"
the pipeline fails with an error indicating $Seed and -Resume are mutually exclusive

scenarios.name: -Resume fails when pipeline.log does not exist

steps.text: Resume-Pipeline fails with an error indicating pipeline.log is missing

scenarios.name: -Resume fails when pipeline.log is empty

steps.text: pipeline.log exists but contains no stage completion markers
Resume-Pipeline fails with an error indicating no completed stages found

scenarios.name: -Resume fails when pipeline.log contains malformed entries

steps.text: pipeline.log exists but contains garbled text with no parseable markers
Resume-Pipeline fails with an error indicating the log is corrupted or unreadable

scenarios.name: -Resume uses the most recent feature when multiple features exist in log

steps.text: pipeline.log contains "STAGE_COMPLETE:3:auth-flow" from a prior run
pipeline.log later contains "STAGE_COMPLETE:2:payment-flow" from a newer run
Resume-Pipeline detects last completed stage as 2

scenarios.name: -Resume rejects pipeline.log with old 8-stage numbering

steps.text: pipeline.log contains "STAGE_COMPLETE:8:auth-flow" from an old 8-stage pipeline run
Resume-Pipeline fails with an error indicating the log uses an incompatible stage format
the error suggests starting a fresh run

scenarios.name: Stage 1 writes a completion marker on success

steps.text: the user starts a fresh run with seed "Build an auth module"

scenarios.name: Stage 2 requires elicitor.md

steps.text: Resolve-PipelineState is called with FromStage 2 and Dir "docs/auth-flow"

scenarios.name: Stage 2 fails if elicitor.md is missing

scenarios.name: Stage 3 requires elicitor.md AND bdd.feature AND a .tla file

scenarios.name: Stage 3 fails if elicitor.md is missing (cumulative)

validation throws an error indicating elicitor.md is missing

scenarios.name: Stage 4 requires bdd.feature AND .tla file AND unified-debate.md

scenarios.name: Stage 4 fails if unified-debate.md is missing

scenarios.name: Stage 5 requires fixture JSON AND unified-debate.md AND bdd.feature AND .tla files

steps.text: feature "auth-flow" has all stage 1-3 artifacts but no "fixtures/auth-flow/bdd/fixture.json"
validation throws an error indicating fixture JSON is missing

scenarios.name: Stage 5 fails if unified-debate.md is missing (cumulative)

scenarios.name: Stage 6 requires implementation-plan.md AND implementation-plan.json

steps.text: the returned state includes ImplPlanMd and ImplPlanJson paths

scenarios.name: Stage 6 fails if implementation-plan.md is missing

validation throws an error indicating implementation-plan.md is missing

steps.text: the returned state includes ImplDebateSession path

scenarios.name: Stage 7 fails if implementation-debate.md is missing

steps.text: feature "auth-flow" has all stage 1-5 artifacts but no "docs/auth-flow/implementation-debate.md"

features.name: Downstream stages are renumbered and wired in the 7-stage pipeline

it does not look for "bdd-debate.md" or "tla-debate.md"

No stage script exists but is uncalled from vibe.ps1

features.name: Unified debate moderator agent prompt is created and referenced

Moderator does not evaluate documents directly

steps.text: the moderator determines consensus based on expert feedback
the moderator does not substitute its own judgment for the experts'

scenarios.name: Moderator uses existing experts directory

steps.text: the unified debate moderator is invoked
it selects experts for the debate
it draws from the existing agents/experts/ directory
no new expert prompts are created for this feature

scenarios.name: Stage 3 references the unified debate moderator prompt

steps.text: the debate moderator file path is inspected

features.name: Full pipeline flows from elicitor through parallel writes, debate, and into implementation

scenarios.name: Elicitor output flows into parallel writers
Parallel writer output flows into unified debate

steps.text: the unified debate loop receives both files for review

scenarios.name: Unified debate output flows into post-debate

steps.text: stage 3 produced final versions of bdd.feature and tla/*.tla
the post-debate stage reads the final bdd.feature to generate fixture JSON

scenarios.name: Post-debate output flows into implementation writer

steps.text: the implementation writer has access to the fixture, both specs, and unified-debate.md

scenarios.name: Resume at stage 3 loads correct state

steps.text: pipeline.log shows stage 2 completed for feature "auth-flow"
Resolve-PipelineState validates elicitor.md, bdd.feature, and .tla file presence

scenarios.name: Resume at stage 4 loads correct state

Resolve-PipelineState validates all cumulative artifacts through stage 3

Resolve-PipelineState validates fixture JSON and unified-debate.md presence

scenarios.name: Fresh run validates stage outputs before proceeding to next stage

steps.text: the user starts a fresh run with seed "Build an auth module"
stage 2 completes but "docs/auth-flow/bdd.feature" was not written (writer bug)
Resolve-PipelineState runs for stage 3 and detects bdd.feature is missing
the pipeline fails with an error instead of passing garbage artifacts forward

features.name: Write-PipelineLog has a single consolidated definition

scenarios.name: Write-PipelineLog is defined only in pipeline-log.ps1

steps.text: the codebase is inspected for Write-PipelineLog definitions
Write-PipelineLog is defined exactly once in "utils/pipeline-log.ps1"
no other file defines a function named Write-PipelineLog

scenarios.name: Write-PipelineLog accepts -Message and -Root parameters

steps.text: "STAGE_COMPLETE:2:auth-flow" is appended to pipeline.log under the given root
the write is atomic (no partial lines from concurrent callers)

scenarios.name: Old Write-PipelineLog with -Color parameter is removed

steps.text: the codebase previously had a Write-PipelineLog in config.ps1 with -Color parameter
callers that used -Color are updated to use the pipeline-log.ps1 signature
